title: oenothera tetragona var. longistipata
intro: Oenothera tetragona var. longistipata, also known as the Long-stippled Evening Primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Onagraceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. The Long-stippled Evening Primrose has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and may be used to treat conditions such as arthritis and eczema. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, salves, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
